description: Improper cryptographic key management refers to the failure to securely generate, store, or use cryptographic keys. This can lead to: - Unauthorized access to sensitive data. - Exploitation of the contract's logic. - Loss of funds or data.
name: Improper Cryptographic Key Management
remediation: - **Use secure key management:** Leverage secure key management systems or libraries. - **Avoid hardcoding keys:** Never hardcode cryptographic keys in the contract. - **Regularly rotate keys:** Periodically update cryptographic keys to reduce risks.
